reviews.author: BBB complainant
reviews.review_text: Sales and Advertising Issues. I am filing a formal complaint against ADESA Las Vegas regarding an unresolved issue involving a vehicle purchase and a missing key.I purchased a REMOVED vehicle from ADESA Las Vegas and worked directly with REMOVED , REMOVED and REMOVED (General Manager). The listing photos clearly show a key inserted in the vehicles ignition. I have submitted these photos as part of this complaint. This proves the vehicle had a key at the time it was listed and advertised.After the sale, I was informed that the key had been lost. Because the key was missing while the vehicle was still in ADESA Las Vegass possession, the vehicle had to be transported as inoperable (INOP). As a direct result of their failure to provide the key shown in the listing, I was charged an additional $500 in transportation REMOVED be clear, the $500 was not for purchasing a replacement key it was an extra transport charge solely because the vehicle could not be moved as operable due to the missing key.Despite presenting the listing photos as proof that the vehicle originally had a key, ADESA Las Vegas has refused to take responsibility. I have made multiple attempts to resolve this matter directly, but my calls have been ignored and I have been given the runaround.The evidence clearly shows the vehicle had a key at the time it was advertised. If the key is now missing, it was lost while under ADESA Las Vegass control. I should not be financially responsible for additional transportation costs caused by their negligence.I am requesting reimbursement of the $500 additional INOP transportation fee that I was forced to pay due to their loss of the key. I also request a formal written response explaining why this issue has not been properly addressed despite clear photographic evidence.I respectfully request the BBBs assistance in facilitating a fair and timely resolution.

reviews.author: Anon, no nickname
reviews.review_text: Best auction app IMO. This is a modern app that is very intuitive to navigate. It takes some getting used to because all the auctions we are used to working with are so behind on technology, but ADESA has really stepped up their game. Their recommendations are spot on with what I’m interested in, their vehicle values are very accurate and the app is really easy to use.

reviews.author: william laymon
reviews.review_text: A great place to buy vehicles at wholesale. Friendly staff in the office and the yard. Fair and trustworthy practices go a long way in this business, that's why they've been around for 30 years!

reviews.author: Mason P
reviews.review_text: Adesa auto auctions consistently falls…. Adesa auto auctions consistently falls short of the standards expected from a major industry player. The lack of thorough, reliable vehicle disclosures creates an environment where buyers are left to absorb avoidable surprises. It reflects a troubling disregard for accuracy and due diligence - two qualities that should be foundational in any operation. Equally concerning is the posture of their customer support and arbitration teams. Communication is slow, solutions are superficial, and there's a pervasive sense that addressing legit issues is viewed as an inconvenience rather than a responsibility. The process feels designed to exhaust the buyer instead of resolve the problem. Overall, Adesa projects the image of a company far more invested in moving inventory than maintaining transparency, accountability, or trust. For professionals who expect consistency and integrity, this auction is not for you. This auction house demands far more patience than it ever earns. My sales rep, Jackson Taylor and his GM, Mark Wilde have mislead me and refused to help in this matter. DO NOT DO BUSINESS WITH THIS AUCTION! They will sell you JUNK under green light and high CR scores

reviews.author: AwesomeGuyObviously
reviews.review_text: As of 11/26/25 this app is great!. Although when the transition happened this app was missing critical features vs the original one, as of today all those features are back in plus a bunch of other quality of life improvements!

reviews.author: Dealer H
reviews.review_text: Every new update make service worse and worse. Unfortunately, every new update from Adesa makes the service worse and causes more trouble for users and dealers. It feels like the developers are intentionally making our lives harder. Whoever designed the mobile version clearly has no idea what wholesale car sales are and has probably never worked with a normal website, because most of the tools and functions available on the regular web version are simply missing in the mobile one. For example, if you’re buying a car on the regular site but suddenly need to switch to the mobile version, none of your data carries over — your lists disappear, and many essential features are gone. Since Adesa launched this new version, I personally stopped buying cars on Adesa. I used to buy exclusively through the mobile app because it was convenient, simple, and accessible. Now it’s completely the opposite — inconvenient and unusable. So I’m switching to work with Manheim instead. If Adesa ever starts thinking about making things easier for its clients and dealers — rather than harder — and begins developing features for dealers instead of against them, maybe I’ll come back. But for now, I’m not interested.

reviews.review_text: They charging $25 a day for storage fee!! That’s not fair, what options do we have if you don’t have forklift!!! At least provide one so we can get a transportation or take it easy on storage fee! I called today to try to get my fee waived but useless and the accountant was mean “WE CHARGE $25 a day” Wake up!! You are not a two yard You need to make it easy for your customers not make it harder I’m done you are no different from copart

reviews.author: GO DUCKS GO GO GO
reviews.review_text: It’s a Cluster. ADESA has some GREAT EMPLOYEES, but unfortunately they are working for a company plagued with greed that has zero concern or respect for their customers. On top of that they keep changing their log in system to the point of ridiculousness. They should scrap their IT department and hire Mr. (Or Miss) Common Sense to save a sinking ship.

reviews.author: CarBuyer 6767
reviews.review_text: Used to be decent. They have made the app significantly more difficult to navigate, used to be able to find what you’d like, now they constantly shove cars down your throat that you don’t want. If you finally figure out the menu and narrow it down to cars you want to see, it crashes every time and that’s just looking at cars, forget bidding on them. Once bidding gets to the last 20 to 30 minutes or so, it slows to an absolute crawl, can’t bid, get alerted you’ve been outbid 20 minutes after the auction has ended. Adesa Clear was a good platform, now the CR’s are complete trash that don’t even represent the cars and if you are able to to see the condition it crashes at the moment you want to bid or you’ve been outbid and it doesn’t let you know. Not sure how they took something that was decent and ruined it completely, but this will likely be the downfall of Carvana, just like the retail cars they sell, not worth the attempt at all.

I've made multiple attempts to contact the Adesa and Headquarter and also the title department to get updates but communication has either been vague or nonexistent. They told me the seller is untouchable. Now they do not want to pay me for the shipping, they want me to take it to a different location so they can save the seller money. This situation has caused a major inconvenience.

I filed a dispute stating that I have never purchased any cars from this location or of this type of vehicle, yet they still want me to pay. reviews.author: pay more attention
reviews.review_text: If it’s not broken don’t fix it!. We have been using the same app for as long as I can remember? Why change it now ? It work perfectly fine on our end and has great features.

reviews.author: dzlddy
reviews.review_text: This app is BUNS. This app is straight dogwater, no organization, pitiful app to go to from the old app. Not to mention, non app specific issues, but there are some shady tactics at adesa where you can be high bidder at live auction yet they somehow sell unit to a lower bidder than you.. Make it make sense, shady auctioneers at adesa, Jacksonville specifically.

reviews.review_text: Not way they are switching to this app. Horrible they are discontinuing the other app and this app is just frustrating, I can’t even look by lane number

reviews.author: mckaw71
reviews.review_text: Dei at its finest. The app is just as bad as the website. Pics got smaller. Can’t see em on my phone unless I click on it. Hard to find the run numbers. Just to find out where the run lists are takes time. Got all this room in the menu, put in a short cut for run lists. No more marketbook or sales history apparently. That’s neat. Can’t go through the run lists after the sale to see sold prices or what sold/didnt sell. Make pics big, run numbers big, year and mileage big, and auto grade big. Make it look like you tried I guess

reviews.author: uncle boogie
reviews.review_text: Mobile app smoothnes. Not sure why but the adesa mobile app just cruises through all tabs of the app and into simulcast mode smoother than a 2001 Lincoln town car on the highway compared to the multi misfiring manheim app that delays,and sometimes freezes freezes while bidding(lost a couple bids thank you) as if lits using dialup technology! Surprised it doesn’t come with the traditional fax machine transmitting noise. Great job adesa mobile app👍🏽

reviews.author: customer
reviews.review_text: I have purchased numerous of cars…. I have purchased numerous of cars online from ADESA clear. They supposed to have a link description of the damages and problems with the car as well as what works. I have purchased cars that they said was in running condition and had few codes which wasn't major. upon paying for the cars, I have run into cars with bad transmissions, some where the sound doesn't work and some, had engines wasn't any good. So, when you do arbitration on the cars that has severe issue that wasn't described they won't honor the mistake of the member of their origination slackness and tells you basically you have to fix the issue yourself. That totally bad business knowing that, if a dealer is purchasing cars at an offsite that no one is allowed on the see or check the car out the information on the car needs to be accurate. These people are rip offs and need to pay for taking folks hard earn money. I paid $7k for a Hyundai Genesis that has a bad head unit that wasn't described in the damages description, and was told by ADESA clear they don't cover the entertainment system. Hyundai dealer said is going to cost $2300 to fix. SMH this extremely bad business Carvana.

reviews.review_text: David. It’s very nice to bid on and find vehicles you want to buy. Good and easy to use.

reviews.author: nozis_way
reviews.review_text: Great & Helpful. It’s great to use the week before each sale, it gives prices of cars sold previously. Allows you to save search results and create lists which gives you daily updates. 10 times easier to use than Manheim’s Application. This app makes my car buying process quicker, easier, and more efficient

reviews.author: BBB complainant
reviews.review_text: Service or Repair Issues. My business and myself, REMOVED have fallen victim to identity theft. From the information provided to me was that a bid was placed on a vehicle through ADESA TAMPA and apparently "won" . It was an online bid, that I have no knowledge of, did not authorize nor get any information about this BID placed until it was "won". I contacted ADESA support, the head office, the seller of the vehicle etc to which all said the bid/sale needed to be VOIDED. However, the only place with the power to do that is ADESA TAMPA. I have completed and provided to them a identity theft report with the REMOVED To which, REMOVED the REMOVED at TAMPA will not do anything to VOID this transaction. Which is what needs to be done as this was not on behalf of me or my company and the vehicle technically hasn't even been purchased yet because money has not been exchanged, which is why it can be voided.

reviews.author: BBB complainant
reviews.review_text: Product Issues. Attention Adesa Memphis Auto Auction/ REMOVED Invoice # REMOVED (Vin last six #'s: REMOVED ) Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I submitted an 'IF' REMOVED . The business contacted me and states the seller wanted $4,000 more than my original offer. I declined. This action closes the REMOVED out entirely and the buyer is no longer contacted in regards to this sale. Adesa's office manager, REMOVED later contacted me stating the seller had accepted my offer which should have been previously closed on our last contact. I then received an invoice for $1400.00 on a sale i declined. Please address this immediately. Please note: I have contacted this business by email and phone, i gave the business 30 days to review and provide any response to my concerns, the business failed to contact me by email or by phone. The only method of contact from the business has been by mailed invoice. (Attached) REMOVED Desired resolution: Remove the negative connotation from WFL Investments account & close the incorrect $1400.00 REMOVED entirely. I hope to continue my good standing/membership with your auction. REMOVED

reviews.author: Melvin Klippenstein
reviews.review_text: amount we pay on auction fees and living in 2023. It's super amazing how dated and how often the app crashes etc. Overall it's okay but please stay on top of it and listen to the complaints and fix it. The location app is not working well. It's zoomed out like crazy and when you try and find a car and than you play with the zoom it gives an error message. Also add the feature that we can message auctioneer from our app in simulcast , same as desktop also add finger print login.

reviews.author: Kelvin1931
reviews.review_text: Great app. Overall, easy to use for new users.

reviews.author: Sandra Yu-Andrishak
reviews.review_text: trustworthy and professional. On Feb 15, I called ADESA Edmonton and and left a message about auction my car. Mr. Lars Eivinsen called me back and asked me to fill out the application and he sent two gentlemen to pick up the car from my place. On Feb 21, Tuesday, Lars called informing me the car was sold at much higher price. I was given the choices to either pick up the cheque myself or by mail. Due to the distance, I asked him to mail the cheque. On March 8, I texted Lars letting him know that I was still waiting for the cheque. Lars texted me to confirm my address; then, he called letting me know he would reprint the cheque and has the cheque delivered to me the same day. Yes, I got the cheque and the bill of sale at 4:05pm, I really appreciate ADESA's car auction service, making car buying/selling easy. With my own experience, I trust the company, especially Mr. Lars Eivinsen. They had done what they promised. Thank you all!

reviews.author: Claude McClain
reviews.review_text: Worst experience I ever had. Worst experience I ever had. The management team has no idea what goes on right under there own noses.The cars are over priced junkers. Which have to be driven across a busy road with no license plate or insurance.
